title: Test challenge
summary: This architecture document outlines the design for a test challenge, detailing the proposed approach, file-level plan, API/interface changes, constraints, SLAs, risks, trade-offs, and edge cases.

artifacts.design_doc_md:
# Architecture Document for Test Challenge

## Problem Summary
This document outlines the architecture for a test challenge aimed at evaluating development skills.

## Proposed Approach
The challenge will be structured around a series of tasks that test various aspects of software development.

## File-Level Plan
- **main.py**: Entry point for the application.
- **utils.py**: Utility functions used across the application.
- **tests/**: Directory containing unit tests for the application.

## API / Interface Changes
- No specific APIs have been defined yet; this will depend on the tasks included in the challenge.

## Constraints & SLAs
- The challenge must be completed within a specified time frame (to be determined).

## Risks & Trade-offs
- Potential risk of participants not having the required knowledge for certain tasks.
- Trade-off between challenge complexity and participant engagement.

## Edge Cases
- Consider scenarios where participants may not have access to certain resources.

## Acceptance Checklist
- [ ] Problem Summary is clear and concise.
- [ ] Proposed Approach is logically structured.
- [ ] File-Level Plan is detailed and actionable.
- [ ] API changes are documented if applicable.
- [ ] Constraints and SLAs are defined.
- [ ] Risks and trade-offs are identified.
- [ ] Edge cases are considered.

assumptions: No specific requirements were provided, so the document is based on standard architecture practices.
The audience has a basic understanding of architecture documentation.

Compile an acceptance checklist to ensure all criteria are met.
validations: Document structure aligns with architecture rubric.

rubric_self_check.overall_notes: The document covers all required sections but could benefit from more specific examples in the proposed approach.
